sections.paragraph:
θερεία, ας, ἡ (subst. from the fem. form of the adj. θέρειος [sc. ὥρα]; Pind. et al.; Aristot.; Diod S; pap; En 2:3; Jos., C. Ap. 1, 79) summertime Hs 4:2 (v.l. θρόνος), 3 and 5 (v.l. θέρος).—DELG s.v. θέρομαι 2.

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

fem. fem. = feminine

adj. adj. = adjective

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 453
